com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXBundle

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXConnectivityObject, IXModuledObject, IXObject, IXWriteableObject

    public interface IXBundle
extends IXModuledObject
    Interface for accessing the nodes, regions, connectors and harness associated with a bundle.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Collection<IXVariance> attributeVariances()
      Get attribute variances.
      java.util.Set<IXBundleRegion> getBundleRegions()
      Get the bundle regions owned by this bundle.
      java.util.Set<IXAbstractConductor> getConductorsAtOffset(double offset)
      Get all the conductors(wires) at a given offset on the bundle.
      java.util.Set<IXConnector> getConnectors()
      Get the connectors attached to this bundle.
      IXHarness getHarness()
      Get the harness to which this bundle belongs.
      double getMaxComplexityDiameter()
      Get an estimate of the bundle's maximum diameter, across all levels in the bundle's harness.
      java.util.Set<IXNode> getNodes()
      Get the nodes attached to this bundle.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXRingTerminal> getRingTerminals()
      Get the ring terminals placed on the bundle on either end of the bundle (start or end node).

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXModuledObject

        getFunctionalModuleCodes, getGeneratedFunctionalModuleCodes, getGeneratedFunctionalModuleCodesExpression, getGeneratedProductionModuleCodes, getGeneratedProductionModuleCodesExpression, getModuleCodeSetter, getProductionModuleCodes, getUserFunctionalModuleCodes, getUserFunctionalModuleCodesExpression, getUserProductionModuleCodes, getUserProductionModuleCodesExpression

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXWriteableObject

        getAttributeSetter

        getAssembly, getDesign, getInstances, getInstances

    • Method Detail

      • getNodes

        java.util.Set<IXNode> getNodes()
        Get the nodes attached to this bundle.
        Returns:
        a Set of nodes associated with the bundle. First two nodes in the set represent start node and end node in that order.

      • getBundleRegions

        java.util.Set<IXBundleRegion> getBundleRegions()
        Get the bundle regions owned by this bundle.
        Returns:
        a Set of bundle regions on the bundle if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getConnectors

        java.util.Set<IXConnector> getConnectors()
        Get the connectors attached to this bundle.
        Returns:
        a Set of the connectors attached to this bundle if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getRingTerminals

        java.util.Set<? extends IXRingTerminal> getRingTerminals()
        Get the ring terminals placed on the bundle on either end of the bundle (start or end node).
        Returns:
        a Set of the ring terminals placed on either end of the bundle if any exists, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getHarness

        IXHarness getHarness()
        Get the harness to which this bundle belongs.
        Returns:
        the owning harness if it exists, null otherwise.

      • getMaxComplexityDiameter

        double getMaxComplexityDiameter()
        Get an estimate of the bundle's maximum diameter, across all levels in the bundle's harness. Applicable only for Integrator
        Returns:
        double The maximum diameter of the bundle.

      • getConductorsAtOffset

        java.util.Set<IXAbstractConductor> getConductorsAtOffset(double offset)
        Get all the conductors(wires) at a given offset on the bundle. Offset is the length from the start node of the bundle to the specified location . For instance, a offset value of 25mm for a 100 mm bundle would define a position on the 1/4th of the bundle.

        If the offset equals zero or bundle length then, all the wires passed through the start or end node of the bundle respectively will be returned.

        Parameters:
        offset - Offset at which the present conductors will be picked.
        Returns:
        Set<IXAbstractConductor> Set of all conductors(wires) present at a given offset in the bundle.

      • attributeVariances

        java.util.Collection<IXVariance> attributeVariances()
        Get attribute variances. Currently support only variant length for bundles in HarnessXC
        Returns:
        a Collection of current attribute variances
